name: Transformer Architecture
shortDesc: The neural network architecture underlying GPT, BERT, and modern LLMs.

steps.label: Input Embedding
steps.desc: Converts input tokens into dense vectors of dimension d_model.

steps.label: Positional Encoding
steps.desc: Injects information about the order of the tokens since the model has no recurrence.

steps.label: Encoder Stack (xN)
steps.desc: A stack of identical layers. Each layer has two sub-layers.

children.label: Multi-Head Self-Attention
children.desc: Allows the model to attend to different positions in the input sequence simultaneously.

children.label: Add & Norm
children.desc: Residual connection followed by Layer Normalisation.

children.label: Feed Forward Network
children.desc: Two linear transformations with a ReLU activation.

steps.label: Output Probabilities
steps.desc: Linear layer followed by Softmax to generate probability distribution over vocabulary.

name: Basic Neural Network
shortDesc: The fundamental building block of Deep Learning: Input Layers, Hidden Layers, and Output Layers.

steps.label: Hidden Layers (Deep)
steps.desc: Layers where computations and feature extraction happen using weights and biases.

steps.label: Output Layer
steps.desc: Produces the final prediction (class or value).

name: RAG Architecture
shortDesc: Retrieval-Augmented Generation: Enhances LLMs by retrieving external data.

steps.label: User Query
steps.desc: The question asked by the user.

steps.label: Retriever
steps.desc: Searches a vector database for relevant documents.

steps.label: Context Injection
steps.desc: Combines the user query with the retrieved documents.

steps.label: LLM Generator
steps.desc: Generates the final answer based on the augmented prompt.
